title: Restricted Airflow
shortDescription: The dryer has detected restricted exhaust airflow.
fullDescription: The AF error code indicates the dryer is detecting restricted airflow through the exhaust vent system. The exhaust temperature is higher than expected because air cannot flow freely. This is a fire safety warning that should be addressed immediately. Speed Queen dryers monitor airflow to protect against lint buildup fires.
symptoms: AF displayed on the control panel
Dryer takes multiple cycles to dry clothes
Humid air in the laundry room

fixSteps.title: Clean the lint filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lint filter and clean all accumulated lint. Wash the filter with warm soapy water if there is fabric softener residue buildup.

fixSteps.title: Inspect vent hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dryer from the wall. Check the vent hose for kinks or crushes. Replace flexible foil or plastic hose with rigid metal duct.

fixSteps.title: Clean the exhaust vent duct
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the vent hose from both ends. Use a dryer vent brush kit to clean the entire duct run from the dryer to the exterior.

fixSteps.title: Check exterior vent hood
fixSteps.instruction: Go outside and verify the vent hood flap opens freely. Remove any lint, debris, or bird nests blocking it.

whenToCallPro: For long or complex vent runs, roof vents, or vents running through walls that cannot be cleaned with a standard brush kit.
videoSearchQuery: Speed Queen dryer AF restricted airflow fix

faq.q: Is AF a fire hazard?
faq.a: Yes, restricted airflow is a leading cause of dryer fires. Address this immediately by cleaning the vent system.

faq.q: How often should I clean my dryer vent?
faq.a: At least once a year. If you do heavy laundry (more than 5 loads per week), clean it every 6 months.

faq.q: Can I use a plastic vent hose?
faq.a: No, use only rigid or semi-rigid metal vent duct. Plastic and thin foil hoses are fire hazards.

title: Power Failure
shortDescription: The dryer lost power during a cycle.
fullDescription: The PF error code indicates the dryer experienced a power interruption while a drying cycle was in progress. This is an informational code, not a hardware failure. The cycle was interrupted and did not complete. Press any button to clear the code and restart the cycle.

fixSteps.title: Clear the code
fixSteps.instruction: Press any button on the control panel to acknowledge and clear the PF code.

fixSteps.title: Restart the cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Select the desired cycle and press Start to resume drying.

fixSteps.title: Check electrical connections
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the power cord is firmly plugged in. Check that the circuit breaker has not tripped.

fixSteps.title: Monitor for recurrence
fixSteps.instruction: If PF occurs frequently without known power outages, have an electrician check the circuit for loose connections or voltage issues.

whenToCallPro: If PF occurs repeatedly without any known power outage, an electrician should inspect the electrical supply.
videoSearchQuery: Speed Queen dryer PF power failure code fix

faq.q: Is PF a serious error?
faq.a: No, PF is informational and simply means the dryer lost power during a cycle. Clear it and restart.

faq.q: Will PF damage my dryer?
faq.a: A single power failure will not damage the dryer. Repeated power surges could affect electronics over time.

faq.q: Does the dryer resume automatically after PF?
faq.a: No, you must manually clear the code and restart the cycle.

title: No Heat Detected
shortDescription: The dryer is running but no heat is being produced.
fullDescription: The nH error code indicates the dryer is running but the temperature sensor has not detected any temperature rise, meaning the heating system is not producing heat. This could be caused by a blown thermal fuse, failed heating element (electric), faulty gas igniter (gas), or a tripped high-limit thermostat.
symptoms: Dryer runs but does not heat
Clothes remain damp after full cycle
nH or E:nH displayed on control
Drum rotates but air is cool

fixSteps.title: Check gas supply (gas dryer)
fixSteps.instruction: For gas dryers, verify the gas supply valve behind the dryer is fully open.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Locate the thermal fuse on the blower housing or exhaust duct. Test with a multimeter for continuity. A blown fuse will read open (infinite resistance).

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element (electric)
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and locate the heating element. Test for continuity with a multimeter. The element should read 10-50 ohms. An open reading means it has burned out.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the thermal fuse, heating element, or gas igniter as needed. Always investigate the root cause of a blown thermal fuse (usually restricted airflow) before replacing.

whenToCallPro: For gas dryer repairs if you are not comfortable working with gas components, or if the root cause of a blown thermal fuse cannot be determined.
videoSearchQuery: Speed Queen dryer nH no heat error fix

faq.q: Why did my thermal fuse blow?
faq.a: The thermal fuse blows when the dryer overheats, usually due to restricted airflow. Always clean the vent before replacing the fuse.

faq.q: Can I run the dryer without the thermal fuse?
faq.a: Never bypass the thermal fuse. It is a critical safety device that protects against fire.

faq.q: How do I know if my dryer is gas or electric?
faq.a: Check behind the dryer: gas dryers have a gas line connection and a standard 120V plug. Electric dryers have a large 240V plug.

title: No Power to Heating Circuit
shortDescription: The dryer is not receiving power to the heating circuit (electric) or has no gas supply (gas).
fullDescription: The nP error code indicates the dryer's control board has detected that the heating circuit is not receiving power. On electric dryers, this typically means one leg of the 240V supply is missing. On gas dryers, it may indicate a gas supply issue or ignition failure.

causes.cause: Tripped circuit breaker on one leg (electric)
Blown thermal fuse cutting heater power
Faulty power cord or terminal connection

symptoms: Dryer motor runs but no heat

fixSteps.title: Check circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: For electric dryers, check the dual circuit breaker at the electrical panel. Even if the breakers appear ON, flip them fully OFF then back ON.

fixSteps.title: Check gas supply (gas dryer)
fixSteps.instruction: For gas dryers, ensure the gas shut-off valve behind the dryer is fully open (parallel to the gas line).

fixSteps.title: Test outlet voltage (electric)
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to test the dryer outlet. Electric dryers should read 240V across the two hot terminals.

fixSteps.title: Inspect power cord and terminal block
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Check the power cord prongs for damage and the terminal block connections inside the dryer for loose or burned wires.

whenToCallPro: If the outlet voltage is incorrect after resetting the breaker, call a licensed electrician.
videoSearchQuery: Speed Queen dryer nP no power to heater fix

faq.q: Why does my dryer run but not heat?
faq.a: On electric dryers, one leg of the 240V supply may be missing. The motor runs on 120V but the heater needs 240V.

faq.q: Can a single tripped breaker cause nP?
faq.a: Yes, electric dryers use a double breaker. If only one side trips, the motor runs but the heater does not.

faq.q: Is nP the same for gas and electric dryers?
faq.a: The code appears on both but the causes differ. Electric dryers typically have voltage issues; gas dryers typically have gas supply issues.

title: Cycle Complete / Done
shortDescription: The drying cycle has completed normally.
fullDescription: The dn display indicator means the drying cycle has finished. This is not an error code but a status indicator letting you know the cycle is complete and the clothes are ready to be removed. Remove the clothes promptly to reduce wrinkling.

causes.cause: N/A - this is a normal status indicator

fixSteps.title: Remove clothes
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dryer door and remove the dried clothes promptly to reduce wrinkling.

fixSteps.title: Clean lint filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the lint filter after every load to maintain proper airflow for the next cycle.

fixSteps.title: Start new load if needed
fixSteps.instruction: Load the next batch of laundry and select the appropriate cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check clothes if still damp
fixSteps.instruction: If clothes are still damp when dn appears on auto-dry cycles, the moisture sensor may need cleaning. Wipe the sensor bars inside the drum with a damp cloth.

whenToCallPro: No service needed. This is a normal operation indicator.
videoSearchQuery: Speed Queen dryer dn display meaning

faq.q: Is dn an error code?
faq.a: No, dn simply means Done. The drying cycle has completed normally.

faq.q: Why do my clothes seem damp when dn shows?
faq.a: The moisture sensors may need cleaning. Wipe the sensor bars inside the drum with rubbing alcohol. Also check that you selected the appropriate dryness level.

faq.q: Can I change the end-of-cycle signal?
faq.a: On most Speed Queen models, you can adjust or turn off the end-of-cycle signal through the control settings. Check your owner's manual.

title: Temperature Too High
shortDescription: The dryer temperature has exceeded the safe maximum.
fullDescription: The E:tH error code indicates the dryer exhaust temperature has exceeded the maximum safe threshold. The dryer will shut off the heating element and continue running the drum and blower to cool down. This is a serious safety warning typically caused by restricted airflow, a failed cycling thermostat, or a shorted heating element.

fixSteps.title: Stop using the dryer immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Allow the dryer to cool down completely before investigating. This is a safety issue.

fixSteps.title: Clean the entire vent system
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the lint filter, vent hose, entire vent duct, and exterior vent hood. Restricted airflow is the most common cause.

fixSteps.title: Test the cycling thermostat
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Locate the cycling thermostat on the heater housing. Test for continuity at room temperature (should have continuity). Replace if open.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Check the heating element for any coils touching the housing or other coils, which would cause a short to ground. Test for continuity to ground (there should be none).

whenToCallPro: If the vent is clean but the error continues, a technician should inspect the thermostat, element, and control system.
videoSearchQuery: Speed Queen dryer E:tH high temperature error fix

faq.q: Is E:tH dangerous?
faq.a: Yes, overheating is a fire hazard. Do not use the dryer until the cause is found and fixed.

faq.q: Can a clogged vent cause E:tH?
faq.a: Yes, a clogged vent is the most common cause. The hot air cannot escape, causing the temperature to rise excessively.

faq.q: Should I replace the thermal fuse after E:tH?
faq.a: Check the thermal fuse with a multimeter. If it has blown, replace it but also fix the root cause to prevent it from blowing again.

title: Temperature Too Low
shortDescription: The dryer temperature is not reaching the expected level.
fullDescription: The E:tL error code indicates the dryer temperature is not reaching the minimum expected temperature within the allowed time. The heating system is either not working or not producing enough heat. This can be caused by a failed thermal fuse, broken heating element, or faulty gas igniter.

causes.cause: Faulty gas igniter or gas valve coils (gas)

symptoms: Dryer runs but produces little or no heat
Clothes take very long to dry

fixSteps.title: Check the thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Locate the thermal fuse on the blower housing or exhaust duct. Test for continuity with a multimeter. Replace if blown (open circuit).

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element (electric)
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the heating element and test for continuity (should read 10-50 ohms). Also test for continuity to ground (there should be none).

fixSteps.title: Test gas igniter (gas dryer)
fixSteps.instruction: Observe the igniter through a small viewport during a cycle. The igniter should glow bright orange. If it glows but gas does not ignite, the gas valve coils may be faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the thermal fuse, heating element, or gas igniter as determined by testing. Always check and clean the vent before replacing the thermal fuse.

whenToCallPro: For gas dryer repairs if you are not comfortable working with gas components.
videoSearchQuery: Speed Queen dryer E:tL low temperature error fix

faq.q: Is E:tL the same as nH?
faq.a: They are related but different. nH means no heat at all. E:tL means some heat may be present but the temperature is too low.

faq.q: Why does the thermal fuse keep blowing?
faq.a: The thermal fuse blows due to overheating, usually from restricted airflow. Always clean the vent system before replacing the fuse.

faq.q: Can I bypass the thermal fuse to test?
faq.a: Never bypass the thermal fuse. It is a one-time safety device that protects against fire. Replace it with the correct part.

title: Drum Fault / Motor Error
shortDescription: The dryer drum is not rotating or the motor has stalled.
fullDescription: The E:dF error code indicates the dryer drum is not rotating as expected. The motor may have stalled, the belt may be broken, or a mechanical obstruction is preventing drum rotation. The control board detected that the motor is not running at the expected speed.
symptoms: Motor hums but drum is stationary
Thumping or squealing sounds before failure

fixSteps.title: Check if drum rotates freely
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dryer door and try to rotate the drum by hand. It should turn smoothly with minimal resistance.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drive belt
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the top panel and front panel. Check if the drive belt is intact and properly routed around the drum, idler pulley, and motor pulley.

fixSteps.title: Replace the belt if broken
fixSteps.instruction: Route the new belt around the drum, thread it through the idler pulley, and loop it around the motor pulley. Ensure proper tension.

fixSteps.title: Inspect drum rollers and motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the belt is intact, check the drum support rollers for wear and the motor for proper operation. Replace worn rollers or a failed motor.

whenToCallPro: If the motor or drum bearings need replacement, as these are complex repairs.
videoSearchQuery: Speed Queen dryer E:dF drum motor error fix

faq.q: How do I know if the belt is broken?
faq.a: If the drum does not rotate but the motor hums, the belt is likely broken. Open the dryer and check visually.

faq.q: Can I replace the belt myself?
faq.a: Yes, belt replacement is a common DIY repair. It requires removing the top and front panels to access the belt path.

faq.q: How long do Speed Queen drive belts last?
faq.a: Speed Queen drive belts typically last 10-15 years in residential use. In commercial settings, they may need replacement sooner.

title: No Heat (Electronic Control)
shortDescription: The electronic control has detected no heat production.
fullDescription: The E:nH error code on newer electronic Speed Queen models indicates no heat is being produced. This is the electronic control version of the nH error. The control board monitors the temperature sensor and has determined that the exhaust temperature has not risen after the heating system was activated.

causes.cause: Faulty gas igniter or valve coils (gas dryer)

symptoms: Drum turns but no heat produced
Clothes remain wet after full cycle

fixSteps.title: Check the vent system
fixSteps.instruction: Before diagnosing components, ensure the vent system is clean. A clogged vent can cause the thermal fuse to blow, resulting in no heat.

fixSteps.title: Test thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Test the thermal fuse with a multimeter. Replace if it shows open circuit. Always investigate why it blew.

fixSteps.title: Test heating element or igniter
fixSteps.instruction: For electric: test the element for continuity (10-50 ohms). For gas: observe the igniter during a cycle - it should glow hot.

fixSteps.title: Replace failed component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the identified failed part. If the thermal fuse blew, clean the vent before replacing to prevent repeat failure.

whenToCallPro: For gas dryer repairs or if the cause of no heat cannot be determined through basic component testing.
videoSearchQuery: Speed Queen dryer E:nH no heat electronic control fix

faq.q: Is E:nH the same as nH?
faq.a: Yes, both indicate no heat. E:nH appears on newer electronic control models while nH appears on older models.

faq.q: What should I check first for no heat?
faq.a: Start with the lint filter and exhaust vent, then test the thermal fuse, then the heating element or gas igniter.

faq.q: Can a dryer work without heat?
faq.a: The dryer will run on air-dry or no-heat settings, but normal drying requires a functioning heating system.

title: Door Open (Electronic Control)
shortDescription: The electronic control has detected the dryer door is open.
fullDescription: The E:do error code on electronic Speed Queen dryers indicates the door switch is reporting the door is not fully closed. The dryer will not start or will stop mid-cycle if the door opens. This can be caused by the door not being fully latched, a faulty door switch, or a broken door catch.

causes.cause: Door not fully closed or latched

symptoms: Dryer stops when door is bumped
Door appears closed but error shows

fixSteps.title: Close door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dryer door fully and close it firmly until you feel the latch click into place.

fixSteps.title: Check the door catch
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the door catch and the strike on the front panel. Check for wear, damage, or misalignment.

fixSteps.title: Test door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the door switch behind the front panel. Test with a multimeter for continuity when the door is closed.

fixSteps.title: Replace door switch
fixSteps.instruction: If the switch fails the continuity test, remove the mounting screws, disconnect the wires, and install a new switch.

whenToCallPro: If the switch and catch appear fine but the error persists, the control board input circuit may need diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Speed Queen dryer E:do door open error fix

faq.q: Is E:do a common error?
faq.a: Yes, door switch issues are among the most common dryer repairs. The switch is inexpensive and easy to replace.

faq.q: Can a worn door catch cause E:do?
faq.a: Yes, if the catch is worn, the door may not push the switch firmly enough. Replace the catch if it is loose or damaged.

faq.q: Where is the door switch located?
faq.a: The door switch is located behind the front panel, near the door opening. It is activated by the door catch when the door closes.
